title: DNA extraction from Atlantic salmon embryos for microsatellite validation of triploidy
author: Callum Howard

steps.title: If eggs stored in ethanol, remove...
steps.instruction: If eggs stored in ethanol, remove using forceps and place on clean tissue to remove excess ethanol.

steps.title: Place embryos in a beaker of...
steps.instruction: Place embryos in a beaker of Tris-HCl ( 5 millimolar (mM) , pH 8 ) for 00:15:00 .

steps.title: Remove the eggs and remove excess liquid with clean tissue
steps.instruction: Remove the eggs and remove excess liquid with clean tissue.

steps.title: For low throughput needs the eggs...
steps.instruction: For low throughput needs the eggs can then be placed into individual 1.5 mL screw cap tubes, for high throughput needs the eggs can be places, one per well, into a 2 mL deep 96-well plate.

steps.title: Pierce the chorion by applying pressure...
steps.instruction: Pierce the chorion by applying pressure using the end of the forceps. Between eggs, the forceps must be wiped clean before being sterilised using 100% ethanol and ddH 2 O.

steps.title: Add 400 µL alkaline lysis buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL alkaline lysis buffer to each tube/well and seal.

steps.title: Invert 5 times, and placed into...
steps.instruction: Invert 5 times, and placed into either a heat block or a laboratory oven running at 90 °C for 00:30:00 .

steps.title: Remove and place On ice for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Remove and place On ice for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Unseal and add an equal amount...
steps.instruction: Unseal and add an equal amount ( 400 µL ) of neutralisation buffer.

steps.title: Reseal and rapidly invert 10 times...
steps.instruction: Reseal and rapidly invert 10 times and then spin down briefly using a centrifuge.

steps.title: Spin down for 14000 rpm, ,...
steps.instruction: Spin down for 14000 rpm, , 30 seconds (or 20,000 g).

steps.title: Collect the middle layer of the solution
steps.instruction: Collect the middle layer of the solution. The bottom layer contains the egg and solid contaminants, while the top layer contains lipid contaminants.

steps.title: The DNA (middle layer) can now...
steps.instruction: The DNA (middle layer) can now be used instantly, stored at 4 °C for up to a week, or stored at -18 °C for use later on.

steps.title: In order to evaluate the effectiveness...
steps.instruction: In order to evaluate the effectiveness of the DNA extraction process and usability of the extracted DNA, a combination of PCR followed by gel electrophoresis and qPCR can be used. A fragment of the Malic Enzyme 2 gene (exon 3; 472 bp) was amplified using primers previously designed and validated [1]. This gene was selected due to its well-established availability and its size being within the range of the microsatellites of interest. Mix 0.5 µL of sample DNA (middle layer), 3 µL MyTaq HS mix (Bioline, USA), 0.6 picomolar (pM) of each primer ( 0.12 µL ) and 2.26 µL ultrapure water in PCR tube or plate ( 10 µL total).

steps.title: Perform PCR at the appropriate thermal...
steps.instruction: Perform PCR at the appropriate thermal cycle for gene of interest.

steps.title: Load 2
steps.instruction: Load 2.5 µL of the PCR product into a 1.25% agarose gel with 5 µL of 1.5× loading dye (ThermoFisher Scientific, UK) in 0.5× TAE buffer.

steps.title: In this case, 38 cycles of...
steps.instruction: In this case, 38 cycles of 95 °C for 00:00:15 , 60 °C for 00:00:15 and 72 °C for 00:00:40 .

steps.title: Migrate the gel with ethidium bromide...
steps.instruction: Migrate the gel with ethidium bromide and visualised under UV in a transilluminator for the quality of bands and the presence of smear or primer dimer.

steps.title: The qPCR reactions were run on...
steps.instruction: The qPCR reactions were run on a QTower 3 (Analytik Jena, Germany) in accordance with the manufacturer’s instructions: Mix 1 µL of sample DNA (middle layer), 5 µL Sensifast SYBR No-ROX kit (Bioline, USA), 1 picomolar (pM) of each primer ( 0.2 µL ), 3.6 µL ultrapure water in qPCR plate.

steps.title: Perform qPCR starting by 95 °C...
steps.instruction: Perform qPCR starting by 95 °C for 00:03:00 followed by the appropriate thermal cycle for gene of interest.

steps.title: In this case, 40 cycles of...
steps.instruction: In this case, 40 cycles of 95 °C for 00:00:15 , 60 °C for 00:00:15 and 72 °C for 00:00:30 .

steps.title: A qualitative assessment of the strength...
steps.instruction: A qualitative assessment of the strength of the band was used to determine the amount of PCR product to be added to the capillary electrophoresis (between 0.5 µL and 1 µL). Mix required quantity of PCR product with 30 µL of sample loading solution (SLS), and 0.35 µL of size standard (WellRED size standard, Eurofins, Germany) and add to well of capillary electrophoresis plate.

steps.title: Top each well off with one drop of mineral oil
steps.instruction: Top each well off with one drop of mineral oil.

steps.title: Run capillary electrophoresis machine (Beckman Coulter...
steps.instruction: Run capillary electrophoresis machine (Beckman Coulter CEQ 8000, Beckman Coulter, USA) according to the manufacturer’s instructions.
